sources.notes: Statement and constraints: adjacent averaging on rational values, sum n up to 1e6.
Official editorial confirms the prefix-sum convex-chain interpretation and the O(n) below-line / adjacent-above-line criterion.
Luogu mirror used to cross-check the statement.
Independent writeup used to cross-check the equivalent zero-sum prefix-sign formulation.

reference_candidates.notes: Candidate Luogu solution page; not used as evidence in this review.

annotation.summary: Given an integer sequence, repeatedly replace adjacent pairs by their average and decide whether the sequence can become nondecreasing.
annotation.constraints: Total n <= 1e6, values <= 1e6; the decision must be linear and use exact integer cross-products rather than floating rationals.
annotation.core_idea: Move to prefix sums b_i. Nondecreasing a is equivalent to the points (i,b_i) forming a lower-convex chain, and averaging a_i,a_{i+1} replaces b_i by the midpoint of neighboring prefix points. If some prefix point is strictly below the chord from (0,0) to (n,b_n), one can flatten both sides almost to straight lines and expand a convex segment outward. Otherwise all points are on or above the chord; then the only possible target is the chord itself, and two consecutive strictly-above points can never both be pushed exactly onto it. Thus the answer is Yes iff there is a below-chord point, or no two consecutive prefix points are strictly above the chord.
tricks: represent adjacent averaging as midpoint relaxation on prefix sums
test position relative to the global chord by cross multiplication
when no point is below the chord, reduce to exact-on-line versus strictly-above-line states

solution_variants.name: prefix-convex-chain
solution_variants.summary: Compute prefix sums and classify each interior prefix point relative to the line between endpoints. A below-line point is immediately feasible; otherwise reject exactly when two consecutive points are both strictly above the line.

solution_variants.name: zero-sum-prefix-sign
solution_variants.summary: Subtract the average slope conceptually so the endpoint sum is zero; then the same criterion becomes a sign-state condition on transformed prefix sums.

tags.evidence: The decisive reduction is that sortedness is lower convexity of the prefix-sum chain, and the operation is midpoint relaxation of one point on that chain.
tags.description: Problems where a sequence or operation is solved by translating it into convex or concave chain geometry or convex-function behavior, without necessarily using a convex hull data structure.
tags.created_reason: Existing math/geometry is broad and math/geometry/convex-hull is too specific; this problem uses convex-chain feasibility rather than hull construction.

tags.evidence: The array operation becomes local replacement of a prefix-sum point, and the O(n) criterion is expressed on prefix sums.
The editorial justifies arbitrary flattening by variance decrease under averaging and convexity of squared distance from the mean.
The total sum and segment means are invariant under operations, determining the global chord and flattening argument.
Feasibility for the below-chord case is proved by explicitly flattening segments and expanding a convex interval.
